meaning: v.扩充,延伸,伸展,扩大[军]使疏开,给予,提供,演化出的全文,<英>[律]对(地产等)估价
examples: The caves extend for some 12 miles.
There are plans to extend the no-smoking area.
Careful maintenance can extend the life of your car.
There were no plans, he said, to extend the life of old coal power stations.
Expect the NFL to extend their collective bargaining agreement before the 2006 season starts.
